title: The State of Food Security and Nutrition in the World 2023
description: Annual UN report finding that between 691 and 783 million people faced hunger in 2022, and that urbanization is transforming agrifood systems in ways that affect food security and nutrition across the rural-urban continuum.

title: World Development Report 2008: Agriculture for Development
description: Landmark World Bank report making the case that agriculture is a fundamental instrument for sustainable development and poverty reduction, particularly for the 75% of the world's poor who live in rural areas and depend on farming for their livelihoods.

title: Global Food Policy Report 2023: Rethinking Food Crisis Responses
description: IFPRI's annual assessment of the global food policy landscape, examining how the food crisis triggered by COVID-19, climate change, and the Ukraine war requires rethinking crisis responses to build more resilient food systems.

title: Climate-Smart Agriculture Sourcebook
description: Comprehensive reference on climate-smart agriculture providing technical guidance on sustainably increasing productivity, building resilience to climate change, and reducing greenhouse gas emissions across crop, livestock, fishery, and forestry systems.

title: Harvesting Prosperity: Technology and Productivity Growth in Agriculture
description: World Bank report examining how agricultural technology and innovation can drive productivity growth in developing countries, analyzing the roles of R&D investment, extension services, ICT adoption, and enabling policy environments.

title: The State of Food and Agriculture 2019: Moving Forward on Food Loss and Waste Reduction
description: FAO report estimating that about 14% of food produced globally is lost between harvest and retail, and providing guidance on priority actions to reduce food loss across supply chains in different country contexts and commodity systems.

title: Enabling the Business of Agriculture 2019
description: Benchmarks the regulatory environment for agriculture in 101 countries, measuring how regulations affect farmers' ability to access seeds, fertilizer, machinery, finance, and markets, and identifying policy reforms that can improve agricultural productivity.

title: Agricultural Extension in Developing Countries
authors: Gershon Feder, Anthony Anderson, Rinku Murgai, Roger Slade
description: Reviews the evolution and effectiveness of agricultural extension systems in developing countries, examining models including training and visit, farmer field schools, and demand-driven approaches, and analyzing the conditions under which extension improves adoption and productivity.

title: The Impact of Agricultural Index Insurance on Smallholder Farmers: A Systematic Review
authors: Ruth Vargas Hill, Miguel Robles, Francisco Ceballos
description: IFPRI systematic review of agricultural index insurance programs, finding that while insurance can encourage investment in higher-return agricultural activities, challenges including basis risk, low demand, and limited distribution channels constrain its impact.

title: Growing Africa: Unlocking the Potential of Agribusiness
description: Analyzes the potential of agribusiness in Sub-Saharan Africa, estimating that the region's food market could grow from $313 billion to $1 trillion by 2030, and identifying investments in infrastructure, technology, and value chains needed to capture this opportunity.

title: From Agriculture to Nutrition: Pathways, Synergies, and Outcomes
description: Examines the pathways through which agriculture affects nutrition outcomes, finding that nutrition-sensitive agricultural programs can improve diets and nutritional status when they are designed with explicit nutrition objectives and target women.

title: Gender in Agriculture Sourcebook
description: Comprehensive sourcebook on gender issues in agriculture, documenting how closing gender gaps in access to inputs, extension, and markets could increase yields on women's farms by 20-30% and raise total agricultural output in developing countries by 2.5-4%.

title: Food Systems for Better Nutrition
description: FAO State of Food and Agriculture report examining how food systems can be reshaped to improve nutrition, analyzing the roles of food production, processing, distribution, and consumption in determining dietary patterns and nutritional outcomes.

title: Smallholder Agriculture and Market Participation: Evidence from Eastern and Southern Africa
description: Examines the barriers that prevent smallholder farmers in Eastern and Southern Africa from participating in agricultural markets, finding that transaction costs, infrastructure gaps, and information asymmetries are critical constraints to commercialization.

title: Global Report on Food Crises 2023
authors: Global Network Against Food Crises, FSIN
description: Annual report on acute food insecurity finding that approximately 258 million people across 58 countries experienced acute food insecurity at crisis levels or worse in 2022, driven by conflict, weather extremes, and economic shocks.

title: Soil Fertility Management in Sub-Saharan Africa
description: Examines soil fertility challenges in Sub-Saharan Africa, where nutrient depletion constrains agricultural productivity, and analyzes integrated soil fertility management strategies combining organic and inorganic inputs with improved germplasm.

title: Land Governance Assessment Framework: Implementation Manual for Assessing Governance in the Land Sector
description: Framework for assessing land governance quality across countries, examining land tenure recognition, land use planning, land tax management, dispute resolution, and the management of public land, with implications for agricultural development.

title: Digital Agriculture: Feeding the Future
description: Examines how digital technologies including mobile platforms, remote sensing, and big data analytics can transform agriculture in developing countries, improving productivity, market access, and risk management for smallholder farmers.

title: Resilience of Smallholder Farming Systems in Sub-Saharan Africa
description: Examines the resilience of smallholder farming systems in Sub-Saharan Africa to climate and economic shocks, identifying diversification, social capital, and access to safety nets as key determinants of household resilience.

title: ICT in Agriculture: Connecting Smallholders to Knowledge, Networks, and Institutions
description: Updated sourcebook on ICT applications in agriculture, documenting how mobile phones, internet platforms, and digital tools are being used to provide smallholders with market information, weather forecasts, extension advice, and access to financial services.

title: High and Dry: Climate Change, Water, and the Economy
description: Analyzes how water scarcity amplified by climate change could constrain economic growth and agricultural production, with some regions seeing growth rates decline by up to 6% of GDP by 2050, and identifies water management policies to mitigate these impacts.

title: Missing Food: The Case of Postharvest Grain Losses in Sub-Saharan Africa
description: Estimates that postharvest grain losses in Sub-Saharan Africa amount to $4 billion annually, enough to feed 48 million people, and identifies improved storage, drying, and handling technologies that can significantly reduce these losses for smallholder farmers.

title: Climate Change 2022: Impacts, Adaptation and Vulnerability
authors: Hans-Otto Pörtner, Debra C. Roberts, Melinda Tignor, Elvira S. Poloczanska, et al. (eds.)
description: IPCC AR6 Working Group II report assessing climate change impacts, adaptation, and vulnerability worldwide, with major findings on the 3.3-3.6 billion people living in highly vulnerable contexts, concentrated in South Asia, sub-Saharan Africa, and Small Island Developing States.

title: Climate Change 2022: Mitigation of Climate Change
authors: Priyadarshi R. Shukla, Jim Skea, Raphael Slade, Al Khourdajie, et al. (eds.)
description: IPCC AR6 Working Group III report on mitigation of climate change, examining sources of global emissions and pathways to reduce emissions across all sectors, emphasizing the need for immediate and deep reductions to limit warming to 1.5 degrees Celsius.

title: Climate Change 2023: Synthesis Report
authors: Hoesung Lee, Jose Romero, et al. (eds.)
description: The IPCC AR6 Synthesis Report integrates findings from all three Working Groups and three Special Reports, summarizing the state of knowledge on climate change, its impacts, risks, and mitigation and adaptation options.

title: Global Warming of 1.5°C: An IPCC Special Report
authors: Valérie Masson-Delmotte, Panmao Zhai, Hans-Otto Pörtner, Debra Roberts, Jim Skea, Priyadarshi R. Shukla, et al. (eds.)
description: IPCC Special Report on the impacts of global warming of 1.5 degrees Celsius above pre-industrial levels, assessing emission pathways, system transitions, and the implications for sustainable development and poverty eradication.

title: The Economics of Climate Change: The Stern Review
description: Landmark independent review for the UK Government arguing that the benefits of strong, early action on climate change far outweigh the costs. Estimates that unmitigated climate change could cost 5-20% of global GDP annually, while mitigation costs could be limited to around 1% of GDP.

title: The Economics of Biodiversity: The Dasgupta Review
description: Independent review commissioned by HM Treasury providing a new economic framework grounded in ecology, finding that natural capital per person declined by nearly 40% between 1992 and 2014, and calling for fundamental changes in how economic success is measured.

title: Temperature Shocks and Economic Growth: Evidence from the Last Half Century
authors: Melissa Dell, Benjamin F. Jones, Benjamin A. Olken
description: Foundational paper demonstrating that higher temperatures substantially reduce economic growth in poor countries by about 1.1 percentage points per 1 degree Celsius rise, with wide-ranging effects on agricultural output, industrial output, and political stability.

title: Global Non-linear Effect of Temperature on Economic Production
authors: Marshall Burke, Solomon M. Hsiang, Edward Miguel
description: Study published in Nature using economic data from 166 countries (1960-2010) showing that economic productivity peaks at 13 degrees Celsius annual average and declines strongly at higher temperatures, projecting unmitigated warming could reduce average global incomes by 23% by 2100.

title: Global Warming Has Increased Global Economic Inequality
description: Open-access PNAS paper finding that global warming has exacerbated global economic inequality by approximately 25% over the past half century, with warming causing 17-30% decreases in per-capita wealth in the world's poorest countries between 1961 and 2010.

title: Social and Economic Impacts of Climate
authors: Tamma A. Carleton, Solomon M. Hsiang
description: Comprehensive review in Science covering key findings on how climatic conditions affect health, economics, conflict, migration, and demographics, showing that current climate conditions impose economic and social burdens rivaling projected end-of-century impacts.

title: Long-Term Macroeconomic Effects of Climate Change: A Cross-Country Analysis
authors: Matthew E. Kahn, Kamiar Mohaddes, Ryan N.C. Ng, M. Hashem Pesaran, Mehdi Raissi, Jui-Chung Yang
description: NBER working paper studying 174 countries from 1960 to 2014, examining how climate change affects long-term economic growth through deviations from historical climate norms and their impact on labor productivity and real output per capita.

title: Climate Change Economics over Time and Space
authors: Per Krusell, Anthony A. Smith Jr.
description: NBER working paper emphasizing spatial heterogeneity in climate impacts, showing that warming in less-developed countries lowers growth by 1.3 percentage points and reverses structural change by increasing the expenditure share of agriculture, magnifying vulnerability.

title: Shock Waves: Managing the Impacts of Climate Change on Poverty
authors: Stéphane Hallegatte, Mook Bangalore, Laura Bonzanigo, Marianne Fay, Tamaro Kane, Ulf Narloch, Julie Rozenberg, David Treguer, Adrien Vogt-Schilb
description: World Bank report examining how climate change could push millions into poverty and demonstrating that rapid, inclusive, climate-informed development can prevent most short-term impacts while pro-poor emission-reduction policies can limit long-term ones.

title: Turn Down the Heat: Why a 4°C Warmer World Must Be Avoided
authors: Potsdam Institute for Climate Impact Research and Climate Analytics (prepared for the World Bank)
description: World Bank report warning that without action the world is on track for 4 degrees Celsius warming by 2100, with devastating consequences including coastal city inundation, food production risks, unprecedented heat waves, and irreversible biodiversity loss disproportionately affecting the poor.

title: South Asia's Hotspots: The Impact of Temperature and Precipitation Changes on Living Standards
authors: Muthukumara Mani, Sushenjit Bandyopadhyay, Shun Chonabayashi, Anil Markandya, Thomas Mosier
description: World Bank flagship report providing granular spatial analysis showing that more than 800 million people in South Asia live in communities projected to become climate hotspots, with most hotspots in inland areas characterized by low living standards and poor connectivity.

title: Groundswell Part 2: Acting on Internal Climate Migration
authors: Viviane Clement, Kanta Kumari Rigaud, Alex de Sherbinin, Bryan Jones, Susana Adamo, Jacob Schewe, Nian Sadiq, Elham Shabahat
description: World Bank report projecting that climate change could force 216 million people across six world regions to migrate within their own countries by 2050, with South Asia facing up to 40 million internal climate migrants driven by water scarcity, declining crop productivity, and sea-level rise.

title: Country Climate and Development Reports (CCDRs)
description: World Bank series of core diagnostic reports integrating climate change and development considerations for individual countries, identifying main GHG sources, climate vulnerabilities, and prioritized actions for South Asian countries including Bangladesh, Pakistan, and Nepal.

title: Adapt Now: A Global Call for Leadership on Climate Resilience
description: Flagship report of the Global Commission on Adaptation finding that investing $1.8 trillion globally from 2020 to 2030 in five key areas including early warning systems, climate-resilient infrastructure, and resilient water resources could generate $7.1 trillion in net benefits.

title: Adaptation Gap Report 2023: Underfinanced. Underprepared.
description: UNEP annual assessment finding that developing countries' adaptation finance needs are 10-18 times greater than international public finance flows, with an estimated adaptation finance gap of $194-366 billion per year and adaptation planning appearing to plateau.

title: Emissions Gap Report 2023: Broken Record
description: UNEP's 14th annual emissions gap report finding that current pledges put the world on track for 2.5-2.9 degrees Celsius warming, requiring emission cuts of 28% for a 2 degrees Celsius pathway and 42% for a 1.5 degrees Celsius pathway by 2030.

title: Global Environment Outlook – GEO-6: Healthy Planet, Healthy People
description: UNEP's most comprehensive assessment of the state of the global environment, produced by 250 scientists and experts, covering all six UNEP regions and emphasizing that a healthy environment is both a prerequisite and foundation for economic prosperity and human wellbeing.

title: State and Trends of Carbon Pricing 2023
authors: World Bank (Shreya Rangarajan, Seoyi Kim, Harikumar Gadde, Sandhya Srinivasan, with Constanze Haug and Benjamin Görlach)
description: World Bank flagship report showing that 23% of global GHG emissions are now covered by 73 carbon pricing instruments, with record revenues of nearly $95 billion, though uptake in emerging and developing economies remains limited compared to high-income countries.

title: Human Development Report 2021/2022: Uncertain Times, Unsettled Lives: Shaping our Future in a Transforming World
description: UNDP report examining how uncertainty from planetary pressures including climate change is reshaping human development, finding that 90% of countries saw HDI reversals in 2020 or 2021 and calling for new approaches to navigate the Anthropocene.

title: Global Assessment Report on Biodiversity and Ecosystem Services
authors: Eduardo S. Brondízio, Josef Settele, Sandra Díaz, Hien T. Ngo (eds.)
description: IPBES landmark assessment finding that around 1 million animal and plant species are threatened with extinction, with current extinction rates 100-1,000 times higher than the baseline rate, driven by changes in land and sea use, direct exploitation, climate change, pollution, and invasive species.

title: The Value of the World's Ecosystem Services and Natural Capital
authors: Robert Costanza, Ralph d'Arge, Rudolf de Groot, Stephen Farber, Monica Grasso, Bruce Hannon, Karin Limburg, Shahid Naeem, Robert V. O'Neill, Jose Paruelo, Robert G. Raskin, Paul Sutton, Marjan van den Belt
description: Seminal Nature paper estimating the value of 17 ecosystem services for 16 biomes at an average of $33 trillion per year (1997 values), demonstrating that ecosystem services are critical to human welfare and substantially exceed global GNP.

title: Global Assessment Report on Disaster Risk Reduction 2022: Our World at Risk
authors: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction (UNDRR)
description: UNDRR flagship report examining how governance systems must evolve to address systemic disaster risks, finding that risk creation is outstripping risk reduction, with disasters impacting LDC economies roughly 10 times worse than the richest countries as a share of GDP.

title: GAR Special Report 2023: Mapping Resilience for the Sustainable Development Goals
authors: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction (UNDRR)
description: UNDRR special report highlighting how resilience investments support the SDGs, finding that early warning system investments triple in benefit in vulnerable contexts and that droughts have already led to 2 billion people under water stress.

title: A Global Just Transition: Background Note for COP27
authors: United Nations Conference on Trade and Development (UNCTAD)
description: UNCTAD background note prepared for COP27 examining the global dimensions of just transition, emphasizing the need for developing countries to receive adequate financial and technical support for decarbonization while addressing structural inequalities.

title: Climate Finance Provided and Mobilised by Developed Countries in 2013-2022
authors: Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD)
description: OECD report confirming that developed countries exceeded the $100 billion annual climate finance goal for the first time in 2022, reaching $115.9 billion, but noting that finance to low-income countries remained relatively low at 10%.

title: State and Trends in Climate Adaptation Finance 2023
authors: Global Center on Adaptation (GCA) and Climate Policy Initiative (CPI)
description: Joint GCA-CPI report finding that adaptation finance flows declined as a share of total climate finance even as adaptation needs are rising, with the private sector financing less than 3% of adaptation activities globally and debt remaining the primary instrument.

title: Aggregate Trends of Climate Finance Provided and Mobilised by Developed Countries in 2013-2020
authors: Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD)
description: OECD report tracking climate finance provided and mobilised by developed countries over 2013-2020, finding that total climate finance amounted to $83.3 billion in 2020 — falling short of the $100 billion annual goal.

title: Policies and Practices of Climate Change Education in South Asia: Towards a Support Framework
description: Open-access paper in npj Climate Action reviewing climate change education policies across South Asian nations, finding that Pakistan, India, Bangladesh, and Sri Lanka have incorporated climate awareness into national policies but require stronger community-level implementation.

title: Climate Change and Food Security in South Asia: The Importance of Renewable Energy and Agricultural Credit
description: Open-access paper in Nature Humanities and Social Sciences Communications analyzing the relationships between climate change, agricultural credit, renewable energy, and food security in South Asia from 1990 to 2021, finding that climate change reduces food availability and increases food insecurity.

title: World Bank Group Climate Change Action Plan 2021-2025: South Asia Roadmap
description: World Bank roadmap outlining how the Bank Group will support climate mitigation and adaptation in South Asia, where climate financing rose from $1.4 billion in FY2017 to $3.7 billion in FY2021, with nearly half of the region's population living in climate hotspots.

title: Firm Adaptation to Climate Change: Evidence from Establishment-Level Data
description: NBER working paper examining firm-level adaptation to climate change, highlighting a global free-rider problem as developing nations prioritize economic growth over decarbonization and emphasizing the growing climate risks faced by firms in developing countries.

title: The Macroeconomic Impact of Climate Change: Global vs. Local Temperature
description: NBER working paper using 173 countries from 1960 to 2019, finding that global temperature rises lead to GDP per capita declines of 12-16% after six years, with effects persisting 8-10 years, comparable in magnitude to severe financial crises.

title: Adaptation to Climate Change
authors: Tamma Carleton, Amir Jina, Michael Greenstone, et al.
description: NBER working paper reviewing the economics of climate adaptation, finding that existing literature offers relatively little guidance for emerging policy and identifying key knowledge gaps in understanding adaptation effectiveness in developing countries.

title: South Asia Disaster Risk Management Programme: Synthesis Report
description: Joint UNDRR-World Bank synthesis report providing comprehensive hazard risk and vulnerability assessment across South Asian countries, establishing baseline disaster risk profiles to inform regional disaster risk reduction strategies.

title: World Development Report 2011: Conflict, Security, and Development
description: Landmark World Bank report examining the links between conflict, security, and development, finding that 1.5 billion people live in countries affected by repeated cycles of violence and that strengthening legitimate institutions and governance is key to breaking these cycles.

title: Global Trends: Forced Displacement in 2022
description: UNHCR annual report documenting that forced displacement reached 108.4 million people by the end of 2022, driven by the war in Ukraine, ongoing conflicts in Syria and Afghanistan, and new displacement crises in the Horn of Africa and Sahel.

title: Pathways for Peace: Inclusive Approaches to Preventing Violent Conflict
description: Joint UN-World Bank study on conflict prevention, finding that prevention is cost-effective and saves lives, and that inclusive development addressing group-based grievances, security sector reform, and strengthened institutions are critical for sustaining peace.

title: Forcibly Displaced: Toward a Development Approach Supporting Refugees, the Internally Displaced, and Their Hosts
description: World Bank report advocating for a development approach to forced displacement, arguing that refugees and IDPs should be seen as a development challenge requiring long-term investment in livelihoods, education, and host community support alongside humanitarian assistance.

title: Conflict and Development: World Bank Strategy for Fragile and Conflict-Affected Situations
description: World Bank Group strategy for fragility, conflict, and violence (2020-2025), outlining how the Bank will scale up engagement in FCV settings through prevention, remaining engaged during crises, and supporting the transition out of fragility.

title: Civil War and Development Policy
description: Analyzes the economic causes and consequences of civil war in developing countries, estimating the cost of a typical civil war at $64 billion and identifying low income, slow growth, and dependence on primary commodities as key risk factors for conflict.

title: The Grand Bargain: A Shared Commitment to Better Serve People in Need
description: Agreement between major humanitarian donors and organizations to reform humanitarian financing and action, with commitments to increase cash programming, support localization, improve transparency, and reduce reporting burdens.

title: Disaster Risk Reduction Including Climate Change Adaptation Over South Asia: Challenges and Ways Forward
authors: Rajib Shaw, Akhilesh Surjan, Gulsan Ara Parvin
description: Reviews disaster risk reduction challenges in South Asia, examining the intersection of climate change adaptation and DRR, and analyzing institutional frameworks, community-based approaches, and policy integration across the region.

title: Breaking the Conflict Trap: Civil War and Development Policy
authors: Paul Collier, V.L. Elliott, Havard Hegre, Anke Hoeffler, Marta Reynal-Querol, Nicholas Sambanis
description: World Bank research report demonstrating how low-income countries are trapped in cycles of conflict and poverty, with each civil war reducing growth and increasing the risk of future conflict, and proposing policy interventions to break these traps.

title: Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction 2015-2030
description: The global framework for disaster risk reduction adopted at the Third UN World Conference, setting seven targets and four priorities including understanding disaster risk, strengthening governance, investing in resilience, and enhancing disaster preparedness.

title: Conflict, Fragility, and Forced Displacement in the Middle East and North Africa
description: Analyzes how conflict and fragility in MENA have reversed development gains, causing massive displacement, economic destruction, and social disruption, and proposing an integrated approach linking humanitarian, development, and peace interventions.

title: Education in Emergencies: Including Everyone
description: The INEE Minimum Standards for Education provide a framework for quality education in emergencies and chronic crises, covering access and learning environment, teaching and learning, teachers and other education personnel, and education policy.

title: Post-Conflict Transitions Working Paper Series: Post-Conflict Economic Recovery
description: Analyzes patterns of economic recovery after civil war, finding that post-conflict economies typically experience a period of rapid growth driven by reconstruction and resource reallocation, and identifying policies that support sustained recovery.

title: Building Resilience to Conflict Through Food-Security Policies and Programs
description: IFPRI report examining how food security interventions can build resilience to conflict, analyzing the bidirectional relationship between food insecurity and conflict and identifying program design features that strengthen household and community resilience.

title: Securing Development: Public Finance and the Security Sector
description: Examines how public finance and security sector spending interact in developing countries, arguing that improved governance and transparency of security expenditure is essential for development and that poorly managed security sectors undermine state legitimacy.

title: Humanitarian Response Index 2011: Focus on Addressing Displacement
description: Assessment of donor governments' humanitarian response performance, evaluating how well major donors adhere to Good Humanitarian Donorship principles including needs-based allocation, timeliness, and support for accountability and learning.

title: The Economics of the Syrian Civil War
description: Estimates the economic destruction caused by the Syrian conflict, finding cumulative GDP losses of $226 billion by 2016, destruction of about a third of the housing stock, and massive human capital depletion through death, displacement, and disrupted education.

title: World Development Report 2021: Data for Better Lives
description: Explores the potential of the changing data landscape to improve lives of poor people, calling for a new social contract that enables data use while ensuring equitable access and fostering trust. Covers data governance, infrastructure, competition, and institutional frameworks.

title: World Development Report 2016: Digital Dividends
description: Examines how digital technologies have spread rapidly but their broader development benefits have lagged behind. Argues that closing the digital divide requires complementary 'analog' reforms in regulation, skills, and institutions.

title: Digital Economy Report 2021: Cross-Border Data Flows and Development
description: Analyzes how cross-border data flows shape the digital economy, noting that the US and China account for 90% of market capitalization of the world's largest digital platforms. Calls for innovative approaches to data governance ensuring equitable benefit distribution.

title: Measuring Digital Development: Facts and Figures 2023
description: ITU's flagship publication tracking global connectivity with estimates on key indicators including infrastructure, affordability, gender divides, and youth internet use. Highlights persistent digital divides leaving low-income countries behind.

title: The State of Mobile Internet Connectivity 2023
description: Reports that 4.6 billion people use mobile internet (57% of global population), but growth has slowed and significant digital divides persist. Rural populations remain 28% less likely to use mobile internet than urban counterparts.

title: United Nations E-Government Survey 2022: The Future of Digital Government
authors: United Nations Department of Economic and Social Affairs
description: The 12th edition assessing digital government across all 193 UN Member States. Finds that least developed countries, particularly in Africa, remain far below the global average in e-government development, underscoring persistent gaps.

title: Open Government Data Report: Enhancing Policy Maturity for Sustainable Impact
description: Provides an overview of open data policies across OECD member and partner countries. Argues that beyond data openness, creating public value requires engaging diverse user communities and integrating open data with broader digital transformation agendas.

title: Open Government for Stronger Democracies: A Global Assessment
description: Assesses the global open government movement, finding that it currently relies heavily on 'impact stories' rather than robust quantitative evidence. Notes the need for better measurements of open government reform impacts.

title: Navigating Algorithm Bias in AI: Ensuring Fairness and Trust in Africa
description: Documents how algorithmic bias exacerbates existing inequalities in developing countries. Argues that African values and ethical principles are currently lacking in global AI ethics discussions and advocates for culturally sensitive AI development.

title: Ethics and Diversity in Artificial Intelligence Policies, Strategies and Initiatives
description: Examines the lack of diversity in AI design, finding that Global North influences have resulted in a homogenization of algorithmic design at odds with cultural philosophies and interests of the Global South. Discusses digital colonialism and algorithmic coloniality.

title: Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
description: The first-ever global normative instrument on AI ethics, adopted by all 193 UNESCO member states. Covers the entire AI lifecycle and addresses issues of transparency, fairness, human oversight, data governance, and inclusive development.

title: AI and Education: Guidance for Policy-Makers
description: Offers guidance on understanding AI and addressing challenges and opportunities in education. Warns that AI might exacerbate rather than reduce existing inequalities and highlights ethical implications including misuse of personal data.

title: Identity Verification Standards in Welfare Programs: Experimental Evidence from India
authors: Karthik Muralidharan, Paul Niehaus, Sandip Sukhtankar
description: Evaluates reforms integrating biometric ID requirements into India's largest social protection program. Finds that corruption fell but with substantial costs to legitimate beneficiaries, with 1.5-2 million people losing access to benefits during the reforms.

title: Building State Capacity: Evidence from Biometric Smartcards in India
authors: Karthik Muralidharan, Paul Niehaus, Sandip Sukhtankar
description: Evaluates biometrically authenticated payments infrastructure in Andhra Pradesh using a large-scale experiment covering 19 million people. Finds the new system delivered faster, more predictable, and less corrupt payments without adversely affecting program access.

title: Identification for Development: The Biometrics Revolution
description: Identifies 160 biometric programs in the developing world providing ID to about a billion people. Argues that identification should be treated as a component of development policy rather than just a cost on a program-by-program basis.

title: Mobile Phones and Economic Development in Africa
authors: Jenny C. Aker, Isaac M. Mbiti
description: Examines the dramatic growth of mobile phone technology in sub-Saharan Africa. Finds tangible economic benefits from reduced communication costs but cautions that phones alone cannot drive development without complementary infrastructure and policies.

title: The Impact of Mobile Money on Poor Rural Households: Experimental Evidence from Uganda
authors: Alfredo Burlando, Michael Kuhn, Silvia Prina
description: World Bank working paper that randomly assigned 329 areas to receive mobile money agents. Found little transformative effect of the rollout, suggesting that policies focused only on agent expansion will not lead to transformative outcomes.

title: Using Publicly Available Satellite Imagery and Deep Learning to Understand Economic Well-Being in Africa
authors: Christopher Yeh, Anthony Perez, Anne Driscoll, George Azzari, Zhongyi Tang, David Lobell, Stefano Ermon, Marshall Burke
description: Shows that deep learning models trained on satellite imagery can explain 70% of the variation in ground-measured village wealth in held-out African countries, outperforming previous benchmarks from high-resolution imagery.

title: Technology as Amplifier in International Development
description: Proposes the 'Law of Amplification': technology's primary effect is to amplify existing human forces. Argues technology cannot substitute for missing institutional capacity, tends to amplify existing inequalities, and works best when amplifying already successful development efforts.

title: The Sustainable Development Goals Report 2024
description: Official UN report monitoring global progress on the 2030 Agenda using the latest available data and estimates. Provides a comprehensive assessment showing that the current pace of change is insufficient to fully achieve the Goals by 2030.

title: Data for Development: A Needs Assessment for SDG Monitoring and Statistical Capacity Development
authors: Jessica Espey, Eric Swanson, Shaida Badiee, Zach Christensen, and others (SDSN)
description: Estimates that IDA-eligible countries will need to spend $1 billion per year to upgrade statistical systems for SDG monitoring. Recommends that donors maintain and increase ODA contributions to statistics while countries mobilize domestic resources.

title: A World That Counts: Mobilising the Data Revolution for Sustainable Development
authors: UN Secretary-General's Independent Expert Advisory Group on a Data Revolution for Sustainable Development
description: Foundational report calling for a 'data revolution' for sustainable development. Describes the opportunity to improve data essential for decision-making and accountability, and calls on governments and the UN to close data access gaps between developed and developing countries.

title: Africa Data Revolution Report 2018: Status and Emerging Impact
description: Undertakes an in-depth critical assessment of the impact of open data in Africa through six qualitative country case studies. Examines whether the data revolution is delivering on its promises for the continent.

title: Open Data Barometer (Fourth Edition)
description: Measures open data prevalence and impact across 115 countries. Finds that fewer than 1 in 10 datasets are fully open, 93% of government data remains closed, and 71% of countries have zero inclusion impact from open data initiatives.

title: Bridging the Digital Gender Divide: Include, Upskill, Innovate
description: Identifies root causes of the digital gender divide including access hurdles, affordability, education gaps, and socio-cultural norms. Notes that women in rural areas of developing countries face persistent structural constraints confining them to technology-poor environments.

title: Digital Gender Divide or Technologically Empowered Women in Developing Countries? A Typical Case of Lies, Damned Lies, and Statistics
description: Extensive empirical study of the digital gender divide in Latin America and Africa finding that when controlling for employment, education, and income, women are actually more active users of digital tools than men. Argues ICT represents a concrete opportunity to tackle gender inequality.

title: Bridging Digital Divides: A Literature Review and Research Agenda for Information Systems Research
description: Literature review identifying gaps in research on digital divides, particularly the lack of studies on broader effects of digital divides on structural transformation in developing countries. Calls for centering the experiences of Global South populations.

title: E-Participation: A Quick Overview of Recent Qualitative Trends
authors: United Nations Department of Economic and Social Affairs
description: UN working paper finding that while e-participation platforms have spread rapidly, their multiplication has not clearly translated into broader citizen participation. Identifies barriers related to technology access, digital skills, and institutional reluctance.

title: Intersections between Civic Technology (Civic Tech) and Governance in Nigeria and South Africa
description: Explores drivers and impact of civic tech ecosystems in Nigeria and South Africa using a social accountability framework. Categorizes civic tech initiatives into citizen engagement, accountability, service delivery, and policy improvement.

title: Blockchain and Economic Development: Hype vs. Reality
description: CGD policy paper providing a clear-eyed view of blockchain technology's development potential. Examines applications in international payments, digital identity verification, property rights, and aid disbursement transparency.

title: Taming the Beast: Harnessing Blockchains in Developing Country Governments
description: Develops a conceptual framework for how governments in developing countries can harness blockchain technology to tackle key development challenges, bringing together technology, development, and state capacity perspectives.

title: Blockchain Technology for Human Development: Not So Fast?
description: Cautions that the most marginalized populations are least likely to benefit from blockchain due to the digital divide. Questions whether developing countries can leverage the technology to leapfrog given limited reliable internet access among the poor.

title: The Experimental Approach to Development Economics
description: Describes how randomized controlled trials can be used to evaluate development interventions including technology programs. Foundational methodological paper by the 2019 Nobel laureates on experimental approaches in development economics.

title: Artificial Intelligence and Its Implications for Income Distribution and Unemployment
description: NBER working paper arguing that developing countries face a stark new set of challenges from AI-driven automation. Shows that concerns about technological progress in developing countries may be justified but that policy measures can mitigate adverse effects.

title: Governance of Data and Data-driven Technology
description: OpenAIR working paper examining tools for monitoring open data availability and use, including the Global Data Barometer. Assesses government performance on data readiness, implementation, and impact across African countries.

title: Poverty Mapping: Innovative Approaches to Creating Poverty Maps with New Data Sources
description: Covers remote sensing and geospatial analysis methods for poverty estimation considering five data sources. Shows evaluators how to leverage different poverty proxies including satellite imagery and machine learning for mapping.

title: Costing a Data Revolution
description: CGD working paper estimating the costs of achieving the data revolution needed for SDG monitoring. Examines the investment required in statistical systems across developing countries to adequately track development progress.

title: Hunger and Public Action
description: Analyzes the role of public action in solving the problem of hunger in the modern world, using the concepts of entitlements and capabilities to explore the interaction of nutritional, economic, social, and political elements. Published as part of WIDER Studies in Development Economics.

title: The Colonial Origins of Comparative Development: An Empirical Investigation
authors: Daron Acemoglu, Simon Johnson, James A. Robinson
description: Exploits differences in European colonial settler mortality rates to estimate the causal effect of institutions on economic performance, showing that extractive colonial institutions persist and explain much of current cross-country income differences.

title: Institutions as a Fundamental Cause of Long-Run Growth
authors: Daron Acemoglu, Simon Johnson, James A. Robinson
description: Develops the empirical and theoretical case that differences in economic institutions are the fundamental cause of differences in economic development, examining quasi-natural experiments including colonization and the division of Korea.

title: The Economic Lives of the Poor
description: Uses household survey data from 13 countries to document how people living on less than $1-2 per day actually live, examining their consumption, work, saving, and borrowing patterns in granular detail.

title: The Miracle of Microfinance? Evidence from a Randomized Evaluation
authors: Abhijit Banerjee, Esther Duflo, Rachel Glennerster, Cynthia Kinnan
description: First randomized evaluation of introducing microcredit in Hyderabad, India, finding that while microfinance increased business investment, it had no significant effects on consumption, health, education, or women's empowerment.

title: Long-term Effects of the Targeting the Ultra Poor Program
authors: Abhijit Banerjee, Esther Duflo, Garima Sharma
description: Studies the 10-year effects of a 'big-push' asset transfer program in India, finding persistent positive effects on consumption, food security, income, and health that grow for seven years and persist through year ten.

title: Women as Policy Makers: Evidence from a Randomized Policy Experiment in India
description: Exploits India's constitutional amendment randomly reserving one-third of village council head positions for women to show that female leaders invest more in public goods aligned with women's concerns, such as drinking water infrastructure.

title: Schooling and Labor Market Consequences of School Construction in Indonesia: Evidence from an Unusual Policy Experiment
description: Evaluates Indonesia's massive INPRES school construction program (61,000 schools built 1973-1978), using regional and cohort variation to estimate returns to education of 6.8-10.6 percent.

title: Monitoring Works: Getting Teachers to Come to School
description: Tests a camera-based monitoring and financial incentive program in 120 rural Indian schools, reducing teacher absence from 42% to 22% and improving student test scores by 0.17 standard deviations.

title: Remedying Education: Evidence from Two Randomized Experiments in India
authors: Abhijit Banerjee, Shawn Cole, Esther Duflo, Leigh Linden
description: Evaluates two randomized interventions in Indian schools -- a remedial education program using community volunteers and a computer-assisted learning program -- finding significant positive effects on student test scores.

title: The Analysis of Household Surveys: A Microeconometric Approach to Development Policy
description: Classic guide to analyzing household survey data in developing countries, covering welfare measurement, poverty, demand analysis, price reform, and saving, reissued with a new preface under a Creative Commons license.

title: Food and Nutrition in India: Facts and Interpretations
description: Reviews evidence on declining calorie intake in India despite economic growth, documenting that Indian anthropometric indicators for both adults and children are among the worst in the world.

title: Counting and Multidimensional Poverty Measurement
description: Proposes the Alkire-Foster methodology for multidimensional poverty measurement, combining an identification method for who is poor with a class of decomposable poverty measures that satisfy key axiomatic properties.

title: Acute Multidimensional Poverty: A New Index for Developing Countries
description: Introduces the global Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) for 104 developing countries using household-level data across health, education, and living standard dimensions, developed for the 2010 Human Development Report.

title: The Idea of Antipoverty Policy
description: Traces the intellectual history of how poverty shifted from being viewed as socially necessary to a social bad, examining how this transformation in thinking shaped the emergence of modern antipoverty policy.

title: Goodbye Washington Consensus, Hello Washington Confusion? A Review of the World Bank's Economic Growth in the 1990s
description: Critically examines the failure of Washington Consensus policies, advocating for diagnostic approaches to development policy rather than one-size-fits-all prescriptions based on trade liberalization and privatization.

title: Globalization, Structural Change and Productivity Growth
description: Documents that structural change since 1990 has been growth-reducing in Africa and Latin America but growth-enhancing in Asia, as labor moved from low to high-productivity sectors in Asia but in the reverse direction elsewhere.

title: Worms: Identifying Impacts on Education and Health in the Presence of Treatment Externalities
description: Landmark randomized evaluation of school-based deworming in Kenya showing that mass treatment reduced absenteeism by 25% and created substantial health externalities among untreated children, making it extremely cost-effective.

title: The Illusion of Sustainability
description: Demonstrates that efforts to replace subsidized deworming with 'sustainable' alternatives were ineffective: cost-recovery reduced take-up by 80%, and health education did not change behavior in Kenyan schools.

title: Creating Markets for New Vaccines Part I: Rationale
description: Proposes advance market commitments as a mechanism to incentivize private vaccine development for diseases of the developing world, arguing that market failures suppress research on malaria, tuberculosis, and HIV.

title: The Long-Term Effects of Africa's Slave Trades
description: Uses historical shipping records and slave ethnicity data to show a robust negative causal relationship between the number of slaves exported from African countries and their current economic performance.

title: Economic Reform and the Process of Global Integration
description: Influential study arguing that trade openness strongly promotes economic growth, documenting the chronology of trade liberalization and finding that open economies grow significantly faster than closed ones.

title: The Microfinance Promise
description: Comprehensive review of microfinance finding that while innovative contractual forms explain high repayment rates, high repayment has not translated into profits, and evidence on poverty impacts is mixed.

title: Do Rural Banks Matter? Evidence from the Indian Social Banking Experiment
description: Shows that India's 1977-1990 bank branch licensing rule, which caused more rural bank openings in financially underdeveloped states, significantly reduced rural poverty through improved access to credit and savings.

title: Land Reform, Poverty Reduction, and Growth: Evidence from India
description: Uses Indian state panel data from 1958-1992 to show that land reforms enhancing tenure security for poor farmers significantly reduced rural poverty, while land ceiling legislation was blocked and had no effect.

title: Institutions and Development: A View from Below
description: Argues for greater exploitation of micro-data and within-country policy variation to understand how specific institutions influence economic activity, bridging the gap between cross-country institutions literature and microeconomic evidence.

title: Why Are Indian Children So Short? The Role of Birth Order and Son Preference
description: Analyzes 174,000 children to show that India's high stunting rate is driven by eldest son preference: Indian firstborns are taller than African counterparts, but subsequent children face a steep height disadvantage linked to gender-biased resource allocation.

title: The Digital Provide: Information (Technology), Market Performance, and Welfare in the South Indian Fisheries Sector
description: Shows that mobile phone adoption by fishermen in Kerala, India dramatically reduced price dispersion, eliminated waste, and increased both consumer and producer welfare, providing a clean test of how information technology improves markets.

title: Misallocation and Manufacturing TFP in China and India
description: Quantifies resource misallocation across manufacturing plants in China and India compared to the U.S., estimating that reallocating resources to equalize marginal products could boost manufacturing TFP by 30-50% in China and 40-60% in India.

title: Entitled to Work: Urban Property Rights and Labor Supply in Peru
description: Evaluates Peru's urban land titling program covering 1.2 million households, finding that property titles substantially increased labor hours, shifted work from home to outside markets, and reduced child labor.

title: Risk and Insurance in Village India
description: Tests the full-insurance model using data from three villages in semi-arid India, finding surprisingly strong consumption smoothing across households despite rejection of perfect risk-sharing, in a foundational study of informal insurance.

title: Top Incomes in the Long Run of History
authors: Anthony B. Atkinson, Thomas Piketty, Emmanuel Saez
description: Surveys evidence on top income shares across 20+ countries over the long run using tax data, documenting dramatic drops during world wars and divergent post-1980 trajectories between Anglo-Saxon and continental European countries.

title: Income Distribution and Macroeconomics
description: First macroeconomic model to show that with credit market imperfections and indivisible human capital investment, the initial wealth distribution affects aggregate output in both the short and long run, generating multiple steady states.

title: Poverty, Inequality, and Growth: Trends, Policies, and Controversies
description: UNU-WIDER working paper reviewing stylized facts on the interplay between poverty, inequality, and growth, evaluating existing trends, resolving controversies, and providing a platform for informed policy debate.

title: Growth, Inequality, and Poverty Reduction in Developing Countries
description: UNU-WIDER working paper presenting global evidence on how income growth and inequality interact to drive poverty changes in developing countries, finding substantial regional variation in the growth-poverty relationship.

title: Inequality and Growth: A Review on a Great Open Debate in Economics
authors: Guilherme Bandeira de Mello, Leonardo Guilherme Dornelles Bortoloni
description: UNU-WIDER working paper comprehensively reviewing the evolving theoretical and empirical literature on the complex relationship between inequality and economic growth, from the classical economists to modern evidence.

title: World Development Report 2004: Making Services Work for Poor People
description: Flagship World Bank report arguing that broad improvements in human welfare require putting poor people at the center of service provision, providing a framework for improving accountability in health, education, water, and sanitation services.

title: Global Poverty Revisited Using 2021 PPPs
authors: Dean Mitchell Jolliffe, Daniel Mahler, Christoph Lakner, Espen Beer Prydz, Nishant Yonzan
description: World Bank Policy Research Working Paper updating global poverty estimates using 2021 PPPs, setting the international poverty line at $3.00/day and documenting the shift of extreme poverty concentration from South Asia to Sub-Saharan Africa.

title: Halving Global Poverty
description: Examines progress toward the Millennium Development Goal of halving global poverty by 2015, analyzing the relative contributions of growth and redistribution across regions and the policy implications for accelerating poverty reduction.

title: Universal Basic Income in the Developing World
authors: Abhijit Banerjee, Paul Niehaus, Tavneet Suri
description: NBER working paper evaluating the case for universal basic income in developing countries, reviewing evidence on cash transfers and assessing design considerations including targeting, conditionality, and fiscal sustainability.

title: The Influence of Randomized Controlled Trials on Development Economics Research and on Development Policy
authors: Abhijit Banerjee, Esther Duflo, Michael Kremer
description: Reviews how randomized controlled trials have transformed development economics research and policy since the early 2000s, documenting the growing share of RCTs in top journals and their influence on development practice.

title: World Report on Disability
description: First comprehensive global report on disability, estimating that over 1 billion people (15% of the world's population) experience some form of disability, and documenting systematic barriers in health, education, employment, and social participation faced by persons with disabilities.

title: Disability Inclusion and Accountability Framework
description: World Bank's framework for mainstreaming disability inclusion across its operations, establishing commitments to collect disaggregated data, consult with disabled persons' organizations, and ensure projects do not exclude people with disabilities.

title: Global Report on Assistive Technology
description: First global report on assistive technology, finding that more than 2.5 billion people need at least one assistive product but that only 1 in 10 people in need have access, with the greatest gaps in low- and middle-income countries and among older persons.

title: Disability and Poverty in Developing Countries: A Multidimensional Study
authors: Sophie Mitra, Aleksandra Posarac, Brandon Vick
description: Cross-country study analyzing the relationship between disability and poverty in 15 developing countries, finding that persons with disabilities are significantly more likely to experience multidimensional deprivation in education, employment, and living standards.

title: Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities (CRPD)
description: The landmark international treaty affirming the rights of persons with disabilities, establishing obligations for states parties to ensure full and equal enjoyment of all human rights, and shifting the paradigm from a medical to a human rights model of disability.

title: Inclusive Education for Students with Disabilities: A Review of the Best Evidence
description: Reviews evidence on inclusive education for students with disabilities in developing countries, finding that inclusive settings can produce equal or better academic and social outcomes when supported by trained teachers, accessible materials, and appropriate accommodations.

title: WHO Global Disability Action Plan 2014-2021: Better Health for All People with Disability
description: WHO action plan setting three objectives for improving health outcomes for persons with disabilities: removing barriers to health services, strengthening rehabilitation and habilitation services, and improving collection of disability-relevant data.

title: Disability and Development: An Overview
description: World Bank overview of disability and development, presenting data showing that persons with disabilities face a 15 percentage point employment gap and are more likely to be poor, and outlining the Bank's approach to disability-inclusive development.

title: Community-Based Rehabilitation: CBR Guidelines
description: Comprehensive WHO guidelines on community-based rehabilitation, providing practical guidance on implementing CBR programs across five domains: health, education, livelihood, social participation, and empowerment of persons with disabilities.

title: The Relationship Between Disability and Poverty: What Is Known and What Does It Mean for Policy?
description: Reviews the evidence on the disability-poverty nexus, finding a robust bidirectional relationship where disability increases the risk of poverty through reduced employment and extra costs, while poverty increases disability risk through poor nutrition and unsafe living conditions.

title: Global Status Report on Road Safety 2018
description: WHO report documenting that 1.35 million people die and millions more sustain serious injuries from road traffic crashes annually, with low- and middle-income countries bearing 93% of fatalities, making road safety a major cause of disability in developing countries.

title: Disability, Poverty, and Schooling in Developing Countries: Results from 14 Household Surveys
description: Analysis of household survey data from 14 developing countries finding that children with disabilities are significantly less likely to attend school, with disability-related enrollment gaps of 10-60 percentage points depending on country and disability type.

title: UN Flagship Report on Disability and Development 2018
description: First UN flagship report on disability and the SDGs, analyzing how persons with disabilities fare across all 17 Sustainable Development Goals and finding that they are disproportionately disadvantaged on most indicators, calling for disability-inclusive SDG implementation.

title: Mental Health and Development: Targeting People with Mental Health Conditions as a Vulnerable Group
description: WHO report examining how mental health conditions contribute to disability and poverty in developing countries, arguing that persons with mental health conditions should be recognized as a vulnerable group in development programming and social protection systems.

title: Disability Measurement and Monitoring Using the Washington Group Questions
description: Guidance on using the Washington Group Short Set of questions for disability measurement in censuses and surveys, providing a standardized approach to identifying persons with disabilities based on functional difficulties in seeing, hearing, walking, cognition, self-care, and communication.

title: Global Burden of Disease Study: Disability Prevalence and Trends
description: Comprehensive analysis from the Global Burden of Disease study documenting disability trends worldwide, finding that years lived with disability have increased substantially since 1990 and that musculoskeletal, mental health, and neurological conditions are leading causes of disability.

title: Rehabilitation 2030: A Call for Action
description: WHO initiative calling for urgent action to strengthen rehabilitation services globally, documenting that an estimated 2.4 billion people live with health conditions that benefit from rehabilitation yet services remain severely inadequate in most low- and middle-income countries.

title: Disability-Inclusive Development Toolkit
description: UK government strategy and toolkit for disability-inclusive development, committing to mainstreaming disability across all development programming and providing practical guidance on inclusive design, reasonable accommodations, and disability data collection.

title: The Extra Costs of Living with a Disability: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
authors: Sophie Mitra, Michael Palmer, Hoolda Kim, Daniel Mont, Nora Groce
description: Systematic review and meta-analysis estimating the extra costs associated with disability, finding that persons with disabilities face additional costs of 12-40% of household income for items including healthcare, assistive devices, transport, and personal assistance.

title: Leaving No One Behind: The Role of Disability Data in Tracking Progress
description: UN analysis of disability data gaps in monitoring the SDGs, finding that most countries lack disability-disaggregated data for most SDG indicators and recommending adoption of the Washington Group questions in national surveys and administrative data systems.

title: World Development Report 2018: Learning to Realize Education's Promise
description: Flagship World Bank report documenting the global learning crisis, finding that schooling does not always lead to learning, and proposing a three-pronged strategy focused on assessing learning, acting on evidence, and aligning actors to make schools work for all learners.

title: The Effect of Girls' Education on Health Outcomes: Fact Sheet
description: Overview of evidence on the relationship between girls' education and development outcomes, showing that each additional year of schooling for girls reduces child mortality, increases future earnings, and lowers rates of child marriage.

title: Making Schools Work: New Evidence on Accountability Reforms
authors: Barbara Bruns, Deon Filmer, Harry Anthony Patrinos
description: World Bank report examining accountability-based education reforms in developing countries, analyzing evidence on school-based management, teacher accountability, school choice, and performance-based incentives across multiple country contexts.

title: Expanding Opportunities for the Next Generation: Early Childhood Development in the Middle East and North Africa
description: Examines early childhood development in the MENA region, documenting how investments in early learning programs can reduce inequality, improve school readiness, and generate significant long-term returns for individuals and economies.

title: The Case for Investing in Girls' Education
authors: Quentin Wodon, Claudio Montenegro, Hoa Nguyen, Adenike Onagoruwa
description: World Bank study estimating the global costs of not educating girls, finding that limited educational opportunities for girls cost countries between $15 and $30 trillion in lost lifetime productivity and human capital wealth.

title: Student Learning in South Asia: Challenges, Opportunities, and Policy Priorities
authors: Halil Dundar, Tara Beteille, Michelle Riboud, Anil Deolalikar
description: Comprehensive analysis of education quality in South Asia, documenting persistently low learning levels despite substantial enrollment gains and analyzing the teacher, governance, and financing challenges underlying the learning crisis.

title: COVID-19 and Education: The Lingering Effects of Unfinished Learning
description: Joint assessment finding that the COVID-19 pandemic increased learning poverty in low- and middle-income countries from 57% to an estimated 70%, with prolonged school closures causing learning losses equivalent to more than a year of schooling in many countries.

title: Education in Sub-Saharan Africa: Comparing Faith-Inspired, Private Secular, and Public Schools
description: Compares the performance of faith-inspired, private secular, and public schools across Sub-Saharan Africa, finding that faith-inspired schools serve a significant share of students, often in underserved areas, and frequently achieve comparable or better outcomes than public schools.

title: The Learning Generation: Investing in Education for a Changing World
authors: International Commission on Financing Global Education Opportunity
description: Report of the Education Commission chaired by Gordon Brown, proposing a financing compact to achieve a 'learning generation' by 2030, calling for increased domestic spending, reformed aid, and innovative financing mechanisms to address the learning crisis.

title: Learning Adjusted Years of Schooling: Defining a New Macro Measure of Education
authors: Noam Angrist, Simeon Djankov, Pinelopi Goldberg, Harry Anthony Patrinos
description: Introduces a harmonized measure of learning-adjusted years of schooling (LAYS) that combines enrollment and learning quality data, revealing that effective years of schooling are about half of enrolled years in many low-income countries.

title: What Matters Most for School Autonomy and Accountability: A Framework Paper
authors: Dina Abu-Ghaida, Sachiko Kataoka, Quentin Wodon
description: Presents a framework for understanding school-level autonomy and accountability mechanisms in developing countries, analyzing how decentralization, community participation, and accountability tools affect education quality.

title: Loud and Clear: Effective Language of Instruction Policies for Learning
description: Examines the evidence on language of instruction policies in developing countries, finding that children learn better when initially taught in their mother tongue and that early-exit transition to a second language can harm learning outcomes.

title: The Global Findex Database 2021: Financial Inclusion, Digital Payments, and Resilience in the Age of COVID-19
authors: Asli Demirguc-Kunt, Leora Klapper, Dorothe Singer, Saniya Ansar
description: World Bank's comprehensive database finding that 76% of adults globally had a bank or mobile money account in 2021, up from 51% in 2011, with mobile money driving inclusion gains in Sub-Saharan Africa, though gender and income gaps persist.

title: State of the Industry Report on Mobile Money 2023
description: GSMA annual report on the mobile money industry, documenting that the number of registered mobile money accounts surpassed 1.6 billion globally, with transaction values exceeding $1.26 trillion, and that mobile money continues to drive financial inclusion in underserved markets.

title: Financial Inclusion: Overview
description: World Bank overview of financial inclusion as a development priority, summarizing the evidence that access to financial services enables poor households to manage risk, invest in opportunities, and build resilience to economic shocks.

title: Microfinance and Poverty: Evidence Using Panel Data from Bangladesh
description: Panel data study finding that microfinance in Bangladesh helped reduce poverty among participants and generated positive spillovers to non-participants in program villages, with larger effects for extreme poverty and for female borrowers.

title: Expanding Access to Finance: Good Practices and Policies for Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises
description: Comprehensive review of policies and practices for expanding financial access for MSMEs in developing countries, analyzing the roles of credit registries, secured transactions, bank competition, and innovative lending technologies in closing the MSME finance gap.

title: Financial Inclusion and Development: A Cross Country Analysis
description: Foundational cross-country analysis using the first Global Findex data to document financial inclusion patterns across 148 countries, identifying income, education, gender, and age as key determinants of financial access and revealing large within-country inequalities.

title: Financial Education Programs and Interventions: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis
description: Meta-analysis of 76 RCTs on financial education, finding that financial education programs have small but significant effects on financial knowledge and downstream financial behaviors, with effects that are larger in lower-income countries.

title: Achieving Universal Financial Access by 2020
description: World Bank overview of its Universal Financial Access 2020 initiative, tracking progress toward the goal of enabling adults worldwide to access a transaction account, and identifying the remaining barriers including documentation, distance, and cost.

title: FinTech and Financial Inclusion in the New Normal
description: Examines how FinTech innovations accelerated by COVID-19 are reshaping financial inclusion, analyzing the roles of digital lending, insurtech, blockchain-based remittances, and regulatory sandboxes in expanding access while managing emerging risks.

title: Remittances and Financial Inclusion: Evidence from El Salvador
description: Examines the relationship between remittances and financial inclusion in El Salvador, finding that remittance recipients are more likely to have bank accounts and that digital remittance channels can serve as an entry point for broader financial service use.

title: The Little Data Book on Financial Inclusion 2022
description: Pocket edition of Global Findex indicators for over 140 economies, providing country-level data on account ownership, digital payment use, savings and borrowing behavior, and the remaining barriers to financial inclusion across regions and demographics.

title: National Financial Inclusion Strategies: Current State of Practice
description: Reviews the experience of over 60 countries that have adopted national financial inclusion strategies, analyzing common elements including targets, coordination mechanisms, and regulatory reforms, and identifying lessons for effective strategy implementation.

title: Women's Empowerment and Economic Development
description: Reviews the two-way relationship between women's empowerment and economic development, arguing the interrelationships are too weak to be self-sustaining and require continuous policy commitment to equality.

title: The Roots of Gender Inequality in Developing Countries
description: Examines whether gender inequality in developing countries is explained by underdevelopment itself or by cultural norms such as patrilocality and concern for women's purity, with particular focus on South Asia.

title: The Economics of Women's Rights
authors: Michele Tertilt, Matthias Doepke, Anne Hannusch, Laura Montenbruck
description: Examines the strong correlation between expansions in women's rights and economic development, exploring the causal pathways between legal equality and economic outcomes.

title: Women's Economic Empowerment and Inclusive Growth: Labour Markets and Enterprise Development
description: Reviews empirical literature on gender and labour markets, finding that formal regular waged work has the greatest transformative potential for women's empowerment, while labour markets are stratified by gender, class, and ethnicity.

title: Gender Awareness in Modeling Structural Adjustment
description: Foundational feminist economics paper critiquing structural adjustment programs for presuming unlimited supplies of unpaid female labour, establishing the framework for gender-aware macroeconomic analysis.

title: Female Labor Force Participation in Developing Countries
description: IZA World of Labor article examining why female labor force participation varies widely across developing countries, identifying key drivers including education, fertility, social norms, and economic structure.

title: Do Labor Market Opportunities Affect Young Women's Work and Family Decisions? Experimental Evidence from India
description: Experimental study showing that providing BPO recruiting services in Indian villages increased women's school enrollment, BMI, and delayed marriage, demonstrating how labor market opportunities shift gender norms.

title: Social Norms as a Barrier to Women's Employment in Developing Countries
description: UNU-WIDER working paper describing how gender norms influence the relationship between economic development and female employment, identifying opportunities for governments to promote attitude change.

title: Global Wage Report 2018/19: What Lies Behind Gender Pay Gaps
description: ILO flagship report providing a critical assessment of gender pay gap measures across 70 countries covering 80% of wage employees worldwide, showing women are paid approximately 20% less than men.

title: Women, Business and the Law 2024
description: Tenth annual study measuring the legal enabling environment for women's economic opportunity across 190 economies, introducing new indicators on safety and childcare and a framework for measuring the implementation gap.

title: Time to Care: Unpaid and Underpaid Care Work and the Global Inequality Crisis
description: Oxfam report calculating the monetary value of women's unpaid care work at $10.8 trillion annually, arguing this sexist economic system that undervalues care work perpetuates both gender and economic inequality.

title: Public Work Programs and Gender-Based Violence: Evidence from India
description: World Bank working paper examining the relationship between public works programs and women's exposure to gender-based violence, using experimental evidence on intimate partner violence.

title: Child Marriage in South Asia: An Evidence Review
authors: UNICEF Regional Office for South Asia
description: UNICEF review of evidence on child marriage drivers and interventions across South Asia's eight countries, where 290 million child brides reside and more than one in four girls marry before age 18.

title: A Profile of Child Marriage in South Asia
description: UNICEF data brief documenting South Asia's global leadership in reducing child marriage, with girls' risk dropping from 39% to 26% over a decade, and identifying education and social protection as critical strategies.

title: Caste, Gender and Jati: An Intersectional Analysis Aimed at Better Targeting Poverty Alleviation and Women's Empowerment in Bihar, Orissa and Tamil Nadu
description: World Bank intersectional analysis of caste and gender in India, examining how hierarchical stratifications determine status, opportunities, and outcomes for women's empowerment and poverty alleviation.

title: Caste-Gender Intersections in Contemporary India
authors: Nandini Manjrekar, Lucinda Ramberg, Clarinda Still (editors)
description: Open-access special issue in South Asia Multidisciplinary Academic Journal arguing that gender and caste subjectivities are simultaneous, linked, and contextual, based on four studies of lived experiences in India.

title: Gender Equality and Social Inclusion in South Asia: An Assessment for Action
description: ADB assessment analyzing nine dimensions of exclusion and vulnerability in South Asia, including gender inequality, disability, social identity, and sexual orientation, with a 10-point operational guide.

title: Framework for Integrating Gender Equality and Social Inclusion in ADB's South Asia Operations
description: ADB framework outlining practical tools for integrating GESI into project design and implementation across Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Maldives, Nepal, and Sri Lanka.

title: Gender Equality and Disability Inclusion: Guidelines to Address the Specific Needs of Women and Girls with Disabilities
description: ADB guidelines addressing how women and girls with disabilities face compounded barriers from poverty, gender, and disability discrimination, with practical strategies for disability-inclusive development.

title: Disability Inclusion in Gender-Based Violence Programming
description: UNFPA report addressing gender-based violence against women and young persons with disabilities, providing guidance on inclusive programming for sexual and reproductive health services in Asia-Pacific.

title: UNDP LGBTI Inclusion Index: Report on the Pilot Implementation
description: UNDP report on the pilot LGBTI Inclusion Index with 51 indicators covering health, education, political participation, economic empowerment, and security, tested across 52 countries.

title: The Women's Empowerment in Agriculture Index
authors: Sabina Alkire, Ruth Meinzen-Dick, Amber Peterman, Agnes Quisumbing, Greg Seymour, Ana Vaz
description: Technical paper documenting the WEAI developed by IFPRI and OPHI, measuring women's empowerment across five domains in agriculture with a Gender Parity Index for intra-household comparisons.

title: Measurement Properties of the Project-Level Women's Empowerment in Agriculture Index
authors: Elena M. Martinez, Hazel J. Malapit, Agnes R. Quisumbing, Greg Seymour, Audrey Pereira
description: Open-access validation study of the pro-WEAI, the project-level adaptation of the Women's Empowerment in Agriculture Index, tested across 13 agricultural development projects in South Asia and Sub-Saharan Africa.

title: SIGI 2023 Global Report: Gender Equality in Times of Crisis
description: Fifth edition of the OECD Social Institutions and Gender Index measuring discriminatory social institutions across 179 countries, finding progress is uneven and discrimination remains highest within the family sphere.

title: Gender Quotas and Female Leadership: A Review
description: World Bank background paper for the WDR 2012 reviewing evidence on gender quotas in political representation, with particular focus on India's panchayat reservation policy and its effects on governance outcomes.

title: Voice and Agency: Empowering Women and Girls for Shared Prosperity
authors: Jeni Klugman, Lucia Hanmer, Sarah Twigg, Tazeen Hasan, Jennifer McCleary-Sills, Julieth Santamaria
description: World Bank report examining constraints on women's voice and agency, covering freedom from violence, sexual and reproductive health rights, land ownership, and collective action, with policy recommendations.

title: Women's Access to Land: An Asian Perspective
description: UN Expert Group Meeting paper examining women's access to land in Asia, analyzing how patriarchal systems manifest as gender discrimination in land ownership, inheritance, and control.

title: Women's Inheritance Rights and Intergenerational Transmission of Resources in India
authors: Klaus Deininger, Aparajita Goyal, Hari Nagarajan
description: World Bank paper assessing India's Hindu Succession Act amendments, finding the reform increased daughters' likelihood of inheriting land by 22 percentage points and increased female educational attainment.

title: Gender and Land Compendium of Country Studies
description: FAO compendium documenting country-level gender dynamics in land ownership and tenure, finding that less than 15% of agricultural landholders globally are women, with particularly stark gaps in South Asia.

title: More Than 100 Million Women Are Missing
