description: Introduces the concept of adaptive social protection, which integrates social protection, disaster risk management, and climate change adaptation to help poor and vulnerable households prepare for, cope with, and adapt to covariate shocks.

title: Universal Basic Income: A Primer
authors: Ugo Gentilini, Margaret Grosh, Jamele Rigolini, Ruslan Yemtsov (eds.)
description: World Bank volume exploring universal basic income from multiple perspectives, examining the concept's history, the evidence from pilot programs, fiscal implications, and how UBI compares to targeted transfers in different developing country contexts.

title: Social Registries for Social Assistance and Beyond: A Guidance Note and Assessment Tool
description: Technical guidance on building social registries that serve as integrated platforms for identifying, registering, and determining the eligibility of potential beneficiaries for social programs, drawing on country experiences across developing regions.

title: Closing the Coverage Gap: The Role of Social Pensions and Other Retirement Income Transfers
description: Examines how social pensions and non-contributory transfers can close pension coverage gaps in developing countries where formal pension systems cover only a fraction of the elderly, analyzing design options, fiscal costs, and poverty impacts.

title: Protecting the Poor: A Microinsurance Compendium
description: ILO compendium on microinsurance for low-income populations, covering health, life, property, and agricultural insurance products, and analyzing the institutional models, distribution channels, and regulatory frameworks that enable insurance to reach the poor.

title: Social Safety Nets in Latin America: Responding to Crises and Building Resilience
authors: Margaret Grosh, Carlos Leite, Matthew Wai-Poi, Emil Tesliuc
description: Analyzes how Latin American social safety net systems responded to the COVID-19 pandemic, documenting rapid expansion of cash transfers that reached 326 million people, and drawing lessons for building more adaptive and shock-responsive social protection systems.

title: India's National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (NREGA): An Assessment
description: Assessment of India's NREGA, the world's largest public works program guaranteeing 100 days of employment per rural household, examining its implementation, impact on poverty and food security, and challenges including leakage and delayed payments.

title: Social Protection in a Changing Climate: Linking Social Protection and Climate Change Adaptation
description: Explores the potential for linking social protection programs with climate change adaptation strategies, arguing that adaptive social protection can help poor and vulnerable populations manage climate risks through a combination of protective, preventive, and promotive measures.

title: Sourcebook on the Foundations of Social Protection Delivery Systems
description: Comprehensive World Bank sourcebook on social protection delivery systems, covering the full delivery chain from outreach and intake through registration, eligibility assessment, enrollment, payment delivery, and grievance mechanisms.

title: Long-Term Impacts of Conditional Cash Transfers: Review of the Evidence
authors: Sarah Baird, Francisco Ferreira, Berk Ozler, Michael Woolcock
description: Reviews the long-term evidence on conditional cash transfers, finding that while CCTs consistently improve short-term school enrollment and health visits, evidence on sustained impacts on learning, earnings, and poverty reduction after program exit is more mixed.

title: Social Protection and Economic Growth in Low-Income Countries
description: Examines the relationship between social protection and economic growth in low-income countries, arguing that social protection programs can promote growth by enabling the poor to invest in productive activities and build human capital.

title: Government Spending on Social Protection in Sub-Saharan Africa
description: Analyzes social protection spending patterns across Sub-Saharan Africa, finding that most countries spend less than 2% of GDP on social safety nets and that fiscal space constraints require efficiency improvements and better targeting to expand coverage.

title: The Role of Digital Technology in Social Protection During and After COVID-19
description: Documents how digital technologies enabled rapid expansion of social protection during COVID-19, including digital registration, mobile money payments, and data analytics for identification, while also highlighting digital exclusion risks.

title: School Feeding Programs: Addressing Malnutrition and Improving Education
description: Reviews the evidence on school feeding programs in developing countries, finding that they can improve school attendance, attention, and nutritional status, and are most effective when targeted at food-insecure areas and combined with other interventions.

title: Reaching the Last Mile: Social Protection for Persons with Disabilities in Low- and Middle-Income Countries
description: Examines how social protection systems can better serve persons with disabilities, analyzing barriers to access including identification challenges, inaccessible registration processes, and inadequate benefit levels that fail to cover disability-related extra costs.

title: Wash'Em: A Handwashing Behaviour Change Approach
description: Rapid behaviour change methodology for handwashing promotion in emergency and development settings, providing tools for identifying handwashing barriers and designing context-appropriate interventions based on behavioural science.

title: Water, Sanitation, and Hygiene (WASH): Evidence, Gaps, and New Approaches
description: WHO guidelines on sanitation and health providing evidence-based recommendations on sanitation interventions, emphasizing the health impact of safely managed sanitation and the need for systemic approaches beyond individual household interventions.

title: Effect of Water, Sanitation, and Hygiene on the Prevention of Diarrhoea in Young Children: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
authors: Lorna Fewtrell, Rachel B. Kaufmann, David Kay, Wayne Enanoria, Laurence Haller, John M. Colford Jr.
description: Meta-analysis of WASH interventions and diarrhoeal disease, finding that handwashing with soap reduces diarrhoea risk by 47%, water quality improvements at point of use reduce risk by 35%, and sanitation interventions reduce risk by 32%.

title: Water Supply, Sanitation, and Hygiene Promotion Interventions and Child Health Outcomes: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis
description: Systematic review and meta-analysis examining the relationship between WASH interventions and child health, finding significant associations between improved sanitation and reduced stunting, and between handwashing promotion and reduced diarrhoea.

title: WASH Benefits Bangladesh: A Cluster-Randomised Trial of the Impact of WASH Interventions on Child Health and Development
authors: Jade Benjamin-Chung, John M. Colford Jr., Andrew Mertens, Alan Hubbard, et al.
description: Major RCT in rural Bangladesh finding that combined water, sanitation, hygiene, and nutrition interventions reduced diarrhoea prevalence and improved linear growth in young children, though individual WASH components alone had limited effects on growth.

title: WASH in Health Care Facilities: Global Baseline Report 2019
description: First global assessment of WASH in health care facilities, finding that one in four health care facilities worldwide lacks basic water services and that 1.8 billion people use facilities without basic water service, undermining infection prevention and quality of care.

title: Hygiene and Health: Systematic Review of Handwashing Practices Worldwide and Update of Health Effects
description: Systematic review of global handwashing practices and their health effects, finding that handwashing rates are very low across all settings and that handwashing with soap can reduce diarrhoeal disease by 42-47%, making it one of the most cost-effective health interventions.

title: Sanitation and Stunting: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis
description: Systematic review examining the relationship between sanitation and child stunting, finding that improved sanitation is associated with reduced odds of stunting, with stronger effects in settings with high open defecation prevalence.

title: Community-Led Total Sanitation: A Mixed Methods Systematic Review of Evidence and Its Quality
authors: Vidya Venkataramanan, Jonny Crocker, Andrew Karon, Jamie Bartram
description: Mixed-methods systematic review of CLTS program evidence, finding that while CLTS can trigger collective action to end open defecation, achieving and sustaining open defecation free status remains a challenge, particularly for the poorest households.

title: Safely Managed Drinking Water: Thematic Report on Drinking Water 2017
description: First global analysis of safely managed drinking water under the SDG framework, establishing baselines and tracking methods for measuring progress toward universal access to safe, affordable, and accessible drinking water.

title: Sustainable Sanitation for All: Experiences, Challenges, and Innovations
authors: Petra Bongartz, Naomi Vernon, John Fox
description: Comprehensive volume exploring the challenges of sustaining sanitation outcomes after initial behavior change, examining issues of equity, inclusion, monitoring, and the political economy of sanitation programs across multiple countries.

title: Water Scarcity, Risk and Vulnerability: An Assessment Under Climate Change
description: Analyzes the relationship between water scarcity, climate change, and economic growth, estimating that water scarcity exacerbated by climate change could reduce GDP growth by up to 6% in some regions by 2050.

title: The True Cost of Poor Sanitation
description: Estimates the global economic losses from inadequate sanitation at $260 billion annually, covering healthcare costs, productivity losses, and premature deaths, and calculates that every dollar invested in sanitation yields a return of $5.50.

title: Menstrual Hygiene Management: Operational Guidelines
description: Provides operational guidance for integrating menstrual hygiene management into WASH programs, covering the provision of menstrual materials, facilities, education, and addressing stigma in schools and communities.

title: Point-of-Use Household Drinking Water Filtration: A Practical, Effective Solution or Scalable Stopgap?
description: Reviews the evidence on point-of-use water treatment, finding that while these technologies can improve microbiological water quality and reduce diarrhoeal disease, challenges in sustained and correct use limit their long-term effectiveness at scale.

title: Effectiveness of Interventions to Promote Handwashing and Other Health Behaviours Before and After COVID-19: A Systematic Review
authors: Jennifer Brogan, Jennie Musto, Val Curtis
description: Systematic review of handwashing promotion interventions before and during COVID-19, examining which behaviour change techniques are most effective at increasing handwashing and sustaining the practice over time.

title: How Does Improving Water, Sanitation and Hygiene Affect Nutrition? A Systematic Review
authors: Alan D. Dangour, Louise Watson, Oliver Cumming, Sonja Y. Hess, et al.
description: Systematic review examining the pathways between WASH improvements and nutritional outcomes in children, finding limited but suggestive evidence that WASH interventions can reduce stunting through reduced enteric infections and environmental enteropathy.

title: Water Safety Plans: Managing Drinking-Water Quality from Catchment to Consumer
description: WHO guidance on developing and implementing Water Safety Plans as a systematic risk management approach to ensuring the safety of drinking water from source to consumer, applicable to water supply systems in both developed and developing country contexts.

title: Rural Water Supply in Africa: Lessons from Implementation Projects
description: Synthesizes lessons from World Bank-supported rural water supply projects in Sub-Saharan Africa, identifying community management capacity, post-construction support, and sustainable financing as critical factors for long-term functionality of water infrastructure.

title: The Handwashing Handbook: A Guide for Developing a Hygiene Promotion Program
description: Practical guide for designing and implementing handwashing promotion programs in developing countries, covering formative research, communication strategy development, partnership building, and monitoring and evaluation of hygiene interventions.

title: Water, Sanitation, Hygiene, and Soil-Transmitted Helminth Infection: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
authors: Matthew C. Freeman, Masa Ogden, Jacqueline Brooker, et al.
description: Systematic review and meta-analysis examining the relationship between WASH and soil-transmitted helminth infections, finding that access to sanitation and safe water is associated with reduced odds of infection with Ascaris, Trichuris, and hookworm.
